title: Definition of criminal conspiracy
content: When two or more persons agree to do, or cause to be done: (1) an illegal act, or (2) an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy: Provided that no agreement except an agreement to commit an offence shall amount to a criminal conspiracy unless some act besides the agreement is done by one or more parties to such agreement in pursuance thereof.

footnotes.text: The naked agreement is enough: If the conspiracy is to commit a substantive crime/offence, no overt physical act is required to complete the liability. The crime is born the exact moment minds meet and agree.

title: Punishment of criminal conspiracy
content: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term of two years or upwards, shall, where no express provision is made in this Code for the punishment of such a conspiracy, be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
